title: campanula scheuchzeri subf. kerneri
intro: Campanula scheuchzeri subf. kerneri, also known as Alpine Harebell, is a flowering plant that belongs to the bellflower family (Campanulaceae). It is a perennial herb native to high-altitude mountainous regions in Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Alpine Harebell can be prepared as herbal tea, infusions, or tinctures to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
